talk_metadata.talk_title: Heterobifunctional small molecules that rewire DNA repair via PARP2‑BET bromodomain proximity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we don't have a structure yet to know exactly how the molecular recognition occurs here"
Speaker noted: "we see almost undetectable PARP1 engagement in these cells with the small molecule PSIP1"
Speaker admitted: "we still have a number of questions as well, so I hope you'll bear with me"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of activity in PARP1‑deficient cells; resistance emerges when PARP2 is knocked out
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "if you knock out PARP2, PSIP1 is no longer able to inhibit the repair of DNA damage"

key_data_gaps: No in vivo efficacy data presented
No structural information on ternary complex

crispro_opportunity.description: PSIP1 activity depends on PARP2; tumors that lose PARP2 become resistant, suggesting a need for biomarkers to select patients with intact PARP2.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "knock out PARP2, PSIP1 is no longer able to inhibit the repair of DNA damage"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model PARP2‑dependent ternary complex formation and predict biomarkers (e.g., PARP2 expression) to stratify patients.
external_validation_needed: PubMed: resistance mechanisms to PARP2‑focused agents

crispro_opportunity.description: SUMO machinery hits (PIAS4, SEMP1) were uniquely sensitizing to PSIP1, indicating a potential combinatorial biomarker or combination partner.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted: "we see really screaming hits on the SUMO machinery... conferring selective cytotoxicity to PSIP1"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate CRISPR screen data with chemical structure to identify SUMO pathway biomarkers for patient selection.
external_validation_needed: CRISPR screen datasets from Broad Institute
Proteomics data on SUMOylation status in tumor samples

unresolved_questions: What is the exact structural basis of the PARP2‑BET bromodomain ternary complex?
How long-lived are the ternary complexes in live cells?
Does PSIP1 affect transcription at DNA damage sites beyond DNA repair inhibition?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Evolution of bifunctional proximity‑pharmacology and clinical translation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the hazard ratio of 0.4 with a very small P value in these patients against standard of care"
Speaker noted: "we see an overall response rate of about 50% in the first phase 1/2"
Speaker admitted: "we still have a number of questions as well"

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Broad second‑line enrollment without biomarker enrichment beyond KRAS G12C status
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Co‑occurring resistance alterations (e.g., KRAS amplification, STK11 loss) not required for entry
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential under‑powering for subgroup analyses of resistance mechanisms

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on covalent binding to G12C; does not address other KRAS mutants (G12D/V) limiting broader applicability
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing broad label in KRAS G12C patients without stratifying for co‑mutations may dilute signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed preclinical data that daxinrasib achieves 50% response in KRAS G12C PDAC, then concluded that the drug will be effective across all KRAS‑mutant cancers despite lack of data on non‑G12C mutants

key_data_gaps: No OS data presented for the registrational trial
Limited biomarker analysis beyond KRAS G12C

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can design bifunctional molecules that extend the KRAS‑targeting chemistry to G12D/V mutants using proximity‑pharmacology.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted daxinrasib as a success story for binding the unbindable KRAS G12C
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Computational modeling of mutant‑specific pockets combined with recruitment of a suitable partner protein to achieve ternary complex formation for G12D/V.
external_validation_needed: Structural data on KRAS G12D/V pockets
Preclinical efficacy data for KRAS G12D/V proximity agents

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify co‑mutations (e.g., STK11, KEAP1) that predict lack of response to daxinrasib and guide combination strategies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned high response rates but did not present biomarker‑stratified outcomes
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate genomic profiling with response data to generate predictive models for patient selection.
external_validation_needed: TCGA analysis of KRAS G12C tumors
Retrospective biomarker analysis from RASalute trials

cited_competitors.name: Revolution Medicines
cited_competitors.context: Developing KRAS G12C inhibitors similar to daxinrasib

unresolved_questions: Will daxinrasib retain efficacy in KRAS G12C tumors with acquired resistance mutations?
What combination partners could overcome resistance in KRAS G12C‑negative subpopulations?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Strategic landscape of induced proximity: discovery platforms and future directions
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we will likely run out of ligands to start putting things together"
Speaker noted: "function‑first screens are attractive but limited by phenotype scalability"
Speaker admitted: "we still have a number of questions as well"

key_data_gaps: No experimental validation of the proposed high‑throughput transcriptional readout
Lack of in vivo proof‑of‑concept for the multiplexed degradation screens

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply CrisPRO’s ratiometric transcriptional activation platform to enable multiplexed, high‑sensitivity screening of degrader libraries across dozens of targets.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker described a transcription‑based inversion assay that amplifies degradation signals for high‑throughput screening
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Leverage CrisPRO’s computational pipeline to design optimal inhibitor‑transcription factor pairs and predict barcode readouts for large‑scale screens.
external_validation_needed: Benchmarking data comparing HiBiT vs. CrisPRO readouts
Pilot screen data in a relevant cancer cell line

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify SUMO pathway inhibitors as potential partners to enhance PSIP1‑like molecules that rely on SUMO machinery for activity.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted SUMO hits (PIAS4, SEMP1) as selective sensitizers in CRISPR screens
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model synthetic lethal interactions between SUMO inhibition and proximity‑pharmacology agents.
external_validation_needed: Preclinical combination studies with SUMO E3 ligase inhibitors
CRISPR validation of SUMO hits in PSIP1‑treated cells

cited_competitors.name: Broad Institute
cited_competitors.context: Provides CRISPR screens and chemical genomics data referenced for assay development

cited_competitors.name: Dan Nomura's lab (Stablx, Vicinitas, Intact Bio)

unresolved_questions: What is the detection limit for protein‑protein interaction strength using the transcriptional activation assay?
Can RNA‑barcode readouts reliably replace fluorescence for >200‑protein multiplexed screens?
